sourceMovie.title: Species
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Species (1995) stuck because it commits to its science fiction premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Roger Donaldson's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The science fiction and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Incredible Hulk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Species clicked for you, The Incredible Hulk (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Species?
faq.answer: Try The Incredible Hulk, Aliens, The Island—each shares DNA with Species while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Species?
faq.answer: Species (1995) is best described as Science Fiction, Horror, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Species part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Species Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
